title: User-story browser QA matrix 2026-05-13
description: Comprehensive user-story browser QA for Substrukt on 2026-05-13. Derive every user-facing flow from routes/templates/docs, create one child issue per user story, verify with a real browser against a local throwaway instance by opening URLs and clicking/navigating/editing, then file bugs/gaps as child issues and fix them in priority order.
close_reason: All child stories verified and bugs fixed

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Completion audit: all 25 child user-story tickets are closed, discovered bugs sk-is6, sk-imn, sk-alx, sk-n6r, sk-h4e, and sk-enu were fixed and closed, browser/API evidence is under target/tmp/qa-user-story, and full gate passed: nix develop -c cargo test (270 unit tests passed, 2 ignored; 213 integration tests passed; doc tests passed).

title: Story: Anonymous user reaches the correct auth setup surface
description: Persona: anonymous visitor. Goal: open the app and reach the correct first-run, login, register, forgot-password, and reset-password screens. Browser verification: start a throwaway instance with and without configured users, open slash, login, register, forgot-password, and invalid reset URL; click visible auth navigation and verify expected redirects or errors.
close_reason: Browser verified anonymous auth/setup surfaces

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser/curl on http://127.0.0.1:3158. Evidence: first-run / returned 503 with create-admin guidance; /register rendered disabled registration with admin invitation copy; /forgot-password and invalid reset token pages rendered. Screenshots: target/tmp/qa-user-story/screenshots/01-empty-first-run.png, 02-register-no-users.png.

title: Story: Admin edits validates and deletes schemas
description: Persona: admin. Goal: edit an existing schema, see validation errors for invalid JSON or schema, and delete a schema. Browser verification: open schema edit, submit invalid JSON, submit a valid update, use Delete confirmation, and verify list or navigation changes.
close_reason: Browser-verified schema validation, update, and delete flows

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified schema edit/validation/delete using a disposable schema. Invalid JSON in the editor surfaces inline JSONEditor validation before save (screenshots 75 and 77). A valid Disposable schema was created (screenshot 76), then updated to Disposable Updated with an added field and reflected as 2 fields in the schema list (screenshot 78). Delete confirmation removed the disposable schema and returned to the list with only Articles remaining (screenshot 79).

title: Story: Editor creates a collection entry from generated fields
description: Persona: editor. Goal: create content using generated controls for primitive, enum, markdown or textarea, object, array, reference, and upload fields. Browser verification: open New Entry, fill fields, add and remove array rows, upload a file where applicable, save, and verify entry appears in list.
close_reason: Browser verified generated content-entry form and create

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: opened Articles > New Entry, generated form rendered Title, Summary, and markdown Body fields from the schema; filled all fields; Create returned to list with first-post draft row. Screenshots: 19-entry-form-filled.png, 20-entry-created-list.png.

title: Story: User browses collection content with search filters sorting and pagination
description: Persona: admin editor viewer. Goal: list entries, search by text, filter published or draft, sort columns, and use pagination when enough entries exist. Browser verification: click schema content, type search, change status filter, click sortable headers, and verify URL and content table update.
close_reason: Browser verified content list search/filter/sort/pagination

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser after seeding 54 entries through the app API token for list volume. Evidence: list paginated at 50 rows with next/prev controls, page 2 showed seed-post-55, search for Seed Post 55 narrowed to one result, Published filter showed 27 of 55 entries, and Title descending sort put Seed Post 54 first. Screenshots: 21-list-page1-pagination.png, 22-list-page2-pagination.png, 23-list-search.png, 24-list-status-filter-published.png, 25-list-sort-title-desc.png.

title: Story: Editor edits publishes unpublishes and deletes an entry
description: Persona: editor. Goal: open an entry, save edits, publish or unpublish with status control, and delete the entry. Browser verification: click Edit, change a field, save, use Publish and Unpublish, return to list, delete with confirmation, and verify feedback.
close_reason: Browser verified edit publish unpublish and delete

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: edited first-post title/summary and saved to list, reopened and published it, then unpublished it, and deleted disposable seed-post-55 through the list delete button plus confirm dialog. Screenshots: 26-entry-edit-filled.png, 27-entry-updated.png, 28-entry-published.png, 29-entry-unpublished.png, 30-entry-delete-before.png, 31-entry-deleted.png.

title: Story: Editor performs bulk content actions
description: Persona: editor. Goal: select multiple entries and bulk publish, unpublish, and delete them. Browser verification: create or select several entries, use select-all and bulk bar actions, confirm delete, and verify selected rows or statuses change.
close_reason: Browser verified bulk publish unpublish and delete

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: selected seed-post-51 and seed-post-53, bulk bar appeared, Publish changed both from draft to published, Unpublish changed both back to draft, and Delete removed both after accepting the confirm dialog. Screenshots: 35-bulk-selected.png, 37-bulk-publish-page2.png, 38-bulk-unpublish-page2.png, 39-bulk-delete-selected.png, 40-bulk-delete-result.png.

title: Story: Editor reviews content history diffs versions and reverts
description: Persona: editor. Goal: inspect version history for an edited entry, compare a prior version to current, and revert. Browser verification: create and edit entry twice, open History, click Compare, return, click Revert with confirmation, and verify restored field values.
close_reason: Browser verified history diff and revert

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: first-post history listed admin_ui snapshot, Compare showed previous First Post / original summary versus current updated fields, and Revert returned to the edit page with Entry reverted and original title/body restored. Screenshots: 32-entry-history.png, 33-entry-diff.png, 34-entry-reverted.png.

title: Story: User manages a single-kind schema entry
description: Persona: editor or viewer. Goal: a single-kind schema redirects to singleton editor; editors can save it and viewers can inspect read-only. Browser verification: create and open single schema, verify content list redirects to singleton editor, save values, log in as viewer and verify no save controls.
close_reason: Browser-verified single schema redirect, editor save, and viewer read-only view

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified single-kind schema flow. Created Site Settings as kind=single (screenshot 80); opening /apps/qa-garden/content/site-settings redirects directly to /_single/edit. Editor saved siteName/tagline values and received Entry updated feedback with Save/Publish controls (screenshot 81). Viewer opens the same singleton editor with saved values, no Save button, view-only footer, and readonly inputs confirmed in HTML (screenshot 82).

title: Story: Editor uploads files through content forms and uses uploads browser
description: Persona: editor. Goal: upload files through content fields, browse upload metadata, filter by filename or schema, and open or download files. Browser verification: upload a file on an entry, open Uploads, search and filter, follow Used By link, click View and Download.
close_reason: Browser-verified upload field, uploads browser, view, used-by, and download flows

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified upload story. Created Media Items upload schema and uploaded README.md through generated content form (screenshots 83-84 via Playwright because agent-browser CDP failed on active file input). Agent-browser verified media item list and edit form show the current README.md upload (screenshots 85-86), uploads browser list/search/schema filter and Used By link (screenshots 87-88), and opening the file content (screenshot 90). Fixed discovered download bug sk-n6r; final Playwright browser click on rendered Download link saved README.md and cmp matched source README.md, screenshot 91.

title: Story: Admin creates edits fires and deletes deployments
description: Persona: admin. Goal: manage deployment targets including webhook URL, auth token actions, draft inclusion, auto-deploy debounce, manual fire, history, and deletion. Browser verification: create deployment, edit token or auto-deploy settings, fire it, inspect feedback and history, delete it.
close_reason: Verified

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Partial verification complete as admin: created QA Deploy with webhook URL https://httpbin.org/status/204, auth token, include drafts, auto-deploy debounce 10s (screenshot 92); auto-deploy fired successfully (HTTP 204). Edited name/token and disabled auto-deploy (screenshot 93). Manual Fire succeeded and Webhook History shows success rows with HTTP 204 (screenshot 94). Leaving deployment in place temporarily for API-token story, then will delete and close this story.

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Completed remaining delete step as admin after API story: confirmed Delete for QA Deploy Edited and the Deployments page flashed deleted and returned to empty state with 0 deployments (screenshot 112). Earlier verification covered create/edit token and auto-deploy settings/manual fire/history in screenshots 92-94, plus later API fire history.

title: Story: Editor can view and fire deployments but cannot manage them
description: Persona: editor. Goal: use deployment fire capability without admin-only create edit delete controls. Browser verification: log in as editor with app access, open Deployments, verify Fire is available, create edit delete are hidden or forbidden, and firing records feedback.
close_reason: Verified

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified as editor using agent-browser: /apps/qa-garden/deployments shows existing QA Deploy Edited with Fire only and no Create/Edit/Delete controls (screenshots 95, 96). Manual Fire succeeded and added a success row in Webhook History with HTTP 204. Direct /deployments/new and /deployments/qa-deploy/edit render 403 Insufficient permissions (screenshot 97); direct POST delete returned 403 and did not delete the deployment.

title: Story: Admin logs in, uses profile menu, toggles theme, and logs out
description: Persona: admin. Goal: authenticate, land on Apps, use the sidebar profile menu, toggle theme, open Profile, and log out. Browser verification: click through login, sidebar user menu, theme toggle, profile link, logout form, then verify apps requires login again.
close_reason: Browser verified admin login/profile/theme/logout

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser on http://127.0.0.1:3158. Evidence: admin logged in from /login to /apps, opened dashboard, used profile menu, toggled theme (data-mode changed to light), opened /settings/profile, logged out, and protected /apps redirected to /login. Screenshots: 03-admin-apps.png, 04-admin-user-menu.png, 05-profile.png, 06-profile-menu.png, 07-logout-login.png.

title: Story: Viewer can read apps schemas content uploads and cannot mutate
description: Persona: viewer. Goal: inspect allowed app content without seeing or using mutation controls. Browser verification: log in as viewer, open app, schemas, content entry, uploads; verify New Edit Delete Publish Bulk Deployment Data Settings controls are absent or forbidden.
close_reason: Browser-verified viewer read-only access and direct mutation forbiddance

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified viewer permissions. Existing screenshots 52/53 show schemas/content read-only with creation and bulk controls absent. Screenshot 55 verifies an entry detail form renders readonly fields with no EasyMDE toolbar or submit/publish controls. Screenshot 56 verifies uploads can be browsed/searched/filtered without upload/delete controls. Screenshot 57 verifies direct /content/articles/new access returns Insufficient permissions. Fixed and closed discovered bug sk-is6.

title: Story: App access controls restrict non-admin users to granted apps
description: Persona: admin editor viewer. Goal: non-admin users only see and access apps granted to them; admins see all apps. Browser verification: create two apps, grant one app to editor or viewer, verify Apps dashboard and direct URL access for allowed versus denied app.
close_reason: Browser-verified allowed and denied app access for non-admin users

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified app access restrictions. Admin created a denied Restricted Lab app (screenshot 68) while QA Garden remained granted to viewer. Viewer Apps dashboard shows only default and QA Garden, not Restricted Lab (screenshot 69). Direct viewer URL to /apps/restricted-lab/schemas returns the app-context 403 page with recovery navigation (screenshot 70). Earlier screenshots 61-63 also verify revoke/regrant behavior after fixing sk-imn.

title: Story: Admin audits activity with filters and pagination
description: Persona: admin. Goal: inspect audit events and filter by action, actor, date presets or ranges, and pagination. Browser verification: generate actions, open Audit Log, use action actor date controls, click pagination when available, and verify table or URL changes.
close_reason: Verified

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified audit log with agent-browser after dynamic-action fix: default page shows distinct actions including deployment_fired and pagination controls (screenshot 99); clicked next page and reached page=2 with 101–101 range (screenshot 100); selected deployment_fired action and saw only deployment_fired rows (screenshot 101); selected api actor and saw API-created rows (screenshot 102); clicked Today date preset and URL/date inputs populated 2026-05-13 (screenshot 103).

title: Story: Admin views and configures backups
description: Persona: admin. Goal: inspect S3 credential status, schedule configuration, recent backup history, and trigger backup feedback. Browser verification: open Backups, save valid config, try trigger when S3 is not configured, and verify disabled or error states.
close_reason: Verified

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified backups as admin with agent-browser: /settings/backups shows S3 credentials missing for all required env vars, Next Scheduled Backup disabled because S3 is not configured, and Back up now is disabled (screenshot 104). Saved valid schedule config with enabled=true, frequency=6h, retention=14; page flashed Backup configuration updated and persisted values while keeping Back up now disabled due missing S3 (screenshot 105). No recent backup history is shown because no backup has run.

title: Story: Error 404 and forbidden pages keep users oriented
description: Persona: authenticated user. Goal: invalid paths, missing apps or schemas, and forbidden admin routes render useful responses with navigation. Browser verification: open unknown URL, missing app URL, and admin-only routes as editor or viewer; verify status text, layout, and recovery navigation.
close_reason: Verified

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified error/recovery flows with agent-browser: unknown route /no-such-page and missing app /apps/missing-lab render the existing 404 shell with Back to Dashboard (screenshots 106-107). Missing app content schema and editor-forbidden admin route initially returned bare text (screenshots 108-109), filed/fixed sk-enu, then rechecked as HTML error shells with status, identity/nav, and Back to Dashboard (screenshots 110-111).

title: Story: API token enables external content API operations
description: Persona: API client using a browser-created token. Goal: create an app API token in the UI and use it for schemas content uploads export import deployment API endpoints. Verification: browser-create token, then exercise API requests with token and confirm unauthorized or forbidden cases for missing or invalid token.
close_reason: Verified

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified app API token created earlier in the browser (qa-token) using curl against http://127.0.0.1:3158/api/v1/apps/qa-garden. Results saved under target/tmp/qa-user-story/api/summary.txt: missing token 401, invalid token 401, valid token against default app 403; schemas 200; schema articles 200; content create/list/get/update/publish/unpublish/delete returned 201/200/200/200/200/200/204 for api-qa-story-1778666119; upload returned 200 with hash 2d73223f7d4a7f7edcc28b42cc6f0d9b27d97f09f4925b96c337d6d41d0f4cfa and downloaded bytes match README.md; export returned 200 with 7018-byte tar.gz; import returned {status: ok, warnings: []}; deployments list returned QA Deploy Edited and fire returned {status: triggered}.

title: Story: User changes password from Profile
description: Persona: authenticated user. Goal: update password from Settings Profile and continue using the account. Browser verification: open profile, submit wrong current password, submit mismatched new passwords, then submit a valid change and verify old password fails while new password logs in.
close_reason: Browser verified profile password change and login effects

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser on http://127.0.0.1:3158. Evidence: profile form rejected wrong current password, rejected mismatched confirmation, accepted password123 -> password456, old password login then failed, and new password login reached /apps. Screenshots: 08-password-wrong-current.png, 09-password-mismatch.png, 10-password-success.png, 11-password-old-login-fails.png, 12-password-new-login-succeeds.png.

title: Story: Admin invites a user and invitee completes registration
description: Persona: admin and invitee. Goal: create a role-specific invitation, open generated invite URL in a fresh browser session, register invited account, consume invite, and grant expected app access. Browser verification: submit Users invite form, copy and open invite URL, fill registration form, verify invitee can log in and sees allowed apps, then verify invitation is gone.
close_reason: Browser verified invite creation and invitee registration

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser using isolated sessions. Evidence: admin created editor invite and copied invite URL, editor accepted invitation in --session editor and reached /apps with editor role and QA Garden visible; admin Users showed no pending editor invite. Also created a viewer invite for downstream permission stories and registered it in --session viewer. Screenshots: 41-invite-editor-form.png, 42-invite-registration-filled.png, 43-editor-registration-apps.png, 44-viewer-registration-filled.png, 45-viewer-registration-apps.png.

title: Story: Admin manages users roles deletions and pending invites
description: Persona: admin. Goal: view registered users, change another user role, delete another user, and revoke pending invitations while self-protection remains enforced. Browser verification: use Settings Users controls, change role select, revoke invite, delete a test user, and verify self role or delete controls are not available.
close_reason: Browser verified user role pending invite and delete management

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: Users page shows admin self row with no role/delete controls, editor/viewer rows have role selectors and delete buttons; changed editor to viewer and back with User role updated feedback; created and revoked pending revoke@example.test invitation; registered tempdelete user then deleted it via confirm dialog. Screenshots: 46-users-registered-role-controls.png, 47-user-role-changed.png, 48-pending-invite-created.png, 49-pending-invite-revoked.png, 50-temp-user-before-delete.png, 51-temp-user-deleted.png.

title: Story: Admin creates and opens an app
description: Persona: admin. Goal: create a new content app with a generated slug and open it from the Apps dashboard. Browser verification: click New App, type name to auto-fill slug, submit, verify app card appears, click it, and verify app-scoped navigation renders.
close_reason: Browser verified app creation and open flow

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: New App form generated slug qa-garden from QA Garden, Create App returned to /apps with QA Garden card, and opening the card navigated to /apps/qa-garden/schemas with app navigation present. Screenshots: 13-new-app-form.png, 14-app-created-dashboard.png.

title: Story: Admin edits app settings access API tokens and deletes apps
description: Persona: admin. Goal: rename an app, grant or revoke app access to non-admin users, create copy and delete an API token, and delete a non-default app. Browser verification: use app Settings forms and destructive confirmation; verify success or error feedback and dashboard state after deletion.
close_reason: Browser-verified app settings, access, token, and app deletion flows

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified app settings: rename state already persisted as QA Garden Lab (screenshots 15 and 58), user access grant/revoke works after fixing sk-imn (screenshots 61-63), API token create/copy display and delete works with throwaway delete-me-token (screenshots 64-65), and a non-default app can be created then deleted with confirmation (screenshots 66-67).

title: Story: Admin exports and imports app data
description: Persona: admin. Goal: download an app bundle and import a bundle only after explicit app-slug confirmation. Browser verification: click Data, download export, choose a bundle file, verify import button and modal enablement, cancel once, then confirm import and inspect result feedback.
close_reason: Browser-verified data export/import gating and import completion

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Browser-verified Data export/import. Download Bundle saved target/tmp/qa-user-story/qa-garden-export.tar.gz (2.1K) from the UI; screenshot 71 captures the Data page. Uploading the bundle enabled Import Bundle, the confirmation modal stayed disabled until typing qa-garden, then enabled Import and Overwrite; cancel hides the modal and leaves the file selected. Confirmed import repopulated cache and returned to the Data page; screenshot 73 captures the post-import page. Note: agent-browser screenshot failed with a CDP internal error while the file input/modal was active, so modal evidence was captured via snapshot rather than a PNG.

title: Story: Admin creates a collection schema in the JSON editor
description: Persona: admin. Goal: create a collection schema with text, textarea or markdown, number, boolean, enum, upload, object, and array fields. Browser verification: click New Schema, use the JSON editor, submit, and verify schema appears in list and content navigation.
close_reason: Browser verified collection schema creation

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Verified with agent-browser. Evidence: opened /apps/qa-garden/schemas/new, edited the JSON editor with a collection schema for Articles using real keyboard events, submitted Create Schema, and landed on /apps/qa-garden/schemas with Articles listed. Screenshots: 18-schema-created-list.png.

title: Invite form leaves browser on POST endpoint after submission
description: Completion-audit browser QA on 2026-05-13: after submitting Settings > Users > Invite User, the page renders the updated users screen but the browser history/current document is tied to /settings/users/invite. Running agent-browser reload produced chrome-error HTTP ERROR 405. Expected: after successful invite creation, refresh/back history should stay on /settings/users or otherwise reload safely. Repro: login as admin, visit /settings/users, submit invite, reload.
close_reason: Fixed by sending HX-Push-Url: /settings/users on boosted invite responses; verified reload in agent-browser stays on /settings/users and renders the Users page.

title: Bug: Live zero-user server does not notice create-admin until restart
description: User reported running create-admin, then being unable to log in while the home URL still said to run create-admin. Code inspection shows run_server initializes has_users once from AllowThem users at startup; create-admin writes to the same SQLite DB from a separate process, but require_auth only reads the cached AtomicBool. The server should re-check the user table while has_users is false so initial setup does not require a restart.
close_reason: Fixed and verified create-admin works without restarting a live zero-user server

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Fixed by refreshing the cached has_users flag in require_auth when it is false. Manual no-restart verification: started a fresh empty server on port 3161, GET / returned 503 create-admin message; ran create-admin against the same --data-dir/--db-path while server kept running; subsequent GET / returned 303 /login and agent-browser opened the login UI without restarting. Evidence: target/tmp/qa-user-story/screenshots/89-create-admin-no-restart-login.png. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt && nix develop -c cargo check passed.

title: Raw app errors lack navigation shell
description: Browser evidence: /apps/qa-garden/content/missing-schema returns a bare 'Schema not found' body (screenshot 108), and editor opening /settings/users returns bare 'Insufficient permissions' (screenshot 109). The error story expects useful responses with layout/navigation/recovery links, like the existing global 404 page (screenshots 106-107). Fix: route app-scoped not-found and forbidden responses through shared error page rendering instead of raw text tuples.
close_reason: Fixed

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Fixed tested raw UI errors by routing them through the shared error page: /apps/qa-garden/content/missing-schema now renders a 404 shell with Admin nav and Back to Dashboard (screenshot 110); /settings/users as editor now renders a 403 shell with editor identity and Back to Dashboard (screenshot 111). Also verified /apps/qa-garden/schemas/missing-schema/edit returns the same 404 shell. cargo check passed after the patch.

title: Audit action filter omits recorded actions
description: Root cause: templates/settings/audit_log.html hardcodes the action <select> options. The current audit log visibly contains deployment_fired, deployment_updated, deployment_auto_fired, app_access_granted/revoked, entry_published/unpublished, password_changed, app_updated, etc., but those values are not selectable, so admins cannot filter many recorded events from the UI. Fix: query distinct actions from audit_log and render them dynamically while preserving selected filter state.
close_reason: Fixed

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Fixed by adding AuditLogger::list_audit_actions(), passing actions into settings/audit_log.html, and rendering action options dynamically. Browser verified deployment_fired and other previously missing actions now appear/select (screenshots 99, 101). Focused test passed: nix develop -c cargo test test_list_audit_actions.

title: Bug: App access form rejects single-user grants
description: Discovered while verifying app access settings. Revoking viewer from QA Garden left only one non-admin access checkbox checked; the Settings access POST failed with 422 before the handler ran because the handler used Form<AccessForm { access: Vec<String> }>. Reworked the route to parse RawForm URL-encoded access keys so zero, one, or many grants are accepted. Evidence: screenshots 61-63 show viewer revoked, dashboard restricted, and direct app URL denied after fix.
close_reason: Fixed with RawForm access parser and browser-verified revoke/direct-deny flow

title: Bug: Viewer entry view renders editable fields
description: During sk-2a4.20 browser QA, viewer session opened /apps/qa-garden/content/articles/first-post/edit via the list View link. Mutation buttons were absent, but generated form fields and EasyMDE toolbar were still enabled, making the read-only view look editable. Evidence screenshot: target/tmp/qa-user-story/screenshots/54-viewer-entry-editable-gap.png. Expected: viewer content detail should render fields as read-only/disabled and suppress editing toolbars where possible.
close_reason: Fixed and browser-verified read-only viewer entry form

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Fixed viewer entry rendering to use read-only generated fields when the role is viewer. Browser re-test on /apps/qa-garden/content/articles/first-post/edit shows readonly title/summary/body controls, no EasyMDE toolbar, no submit/publish actions, and the view-only footer. Evidence: target/tmp/qa-user-story/screenshots/55-viewer-entry-readonly-fixed.png. Verification: nix develop -c cargo check passed; focused cargo test is currently blocked by unrelated src/email.rs allowthem_core::EmailMessage API drift.

title: QA run: browser dogfood 2026-05-13
description: Browser-driven QA run for Substrukt using agent-browser against a local throwaway data directory. Scope: first-run setup, apps dashboard, user invitation, signup URL/token flow, and obvious UI/UX issues found while using the product as a normal user.
close_reason: Completed browser QA loop for first-admin and invite-token auth flows; fixed the blocking invite and public-registration issues.

title: Build fails against local allowthem EmailSender API
description: Discovered while launching local server for browser QA.  fails in src/email.rs because local ../allowthem has changed EmailSender::send to accept &EmailMessage, and EmailMessage now exposes to/subject/template rather than body/html. Fix is required before product QA can continue.
close_reason: Fixed local allowthem EmailSender and lifecycle event API drift; cargo check --target-dir target passes.

title: Local server cannot listen under sandbox
description: During browser QA,  built successfully but exited with Operation not permitted at src/main.rs:392 while binding/listening on localhost. This is an environment/sandbox blocker; rerun server outside sandbox/escalated for QA.
close_reason: Environment-only blocker resolved by running target/debug/substrukt serve with escalation; server is listening on port 3157.

title: Public registration is available after setup
description: Browser QA shows /register remains publicly available after the first admin exists. /register?token=... uses the generic AllowThem registration form and created a user without consuming the pending invite or assigning/granting the invite's role/access. Need confirm /register without token is also public and gate registration behind a valid invitation or first-run no-users state. Evidence overlaps sk-xxn screenshots in docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/.
close_reason: Fixed by disabling public registration unless --enable-registrations is set while preserving valid invitation-token registration.

title: Bug: Uploads Download link opens inline file instead of downloading
description: Discovered while verifying sk-2a4.17. The uploads table renders View and Download with the same /uploads/file/:hash/:filename href; the server always sends Content-Disposition: inline. In browser automation, clicking Download navigated to the README.md file URL instead of emitting a download, so the control behaves like View. Add an attachment download route or otherwise send Content-Disposition: attachment for the Download action, then re-test with Playwright/agent-browser.
close_reason: Fixed and browser-verified native upload download behavior

comments.author: nambiar
comments.text: Fixed by adding a dedicated /uploads/file/:hash/:filename/download route that serves the file with Content-Disposition: attachment, and by marking View/Download links hx-boost=false so htmx does not intercept native file actions. Browser verification: Playwright clicked the rendered Download link, received a download event, saved README.md, and cmp matched it to the source README.md (11666 bytes). Evidence screenshot: target/tmp/qa-user-story/screenshots/91-upload-download-fixed-list.png. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt && nix develop -c cargo check passed.

title: Invite flow shows a signup URL that 404s
description: Browser-confirmed invite flow bug. Admin Users page displays a generated invite URL at /signup?token=... after POST /settings/users/invite. Opening that URL in a fresh invitee browser session returns Substrukt 404, screenshot: docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/screenshots/invite-url-404.png. Trying /register?token=... instead shows a generic create-account form; submitting the invited email creates a user but does not consume the pending invitation or grant app access. Invitee lands at /apps with NO ACCESS, screenshot: docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/screenshots/register-token-result.png. Admin Users still shows invitee@example.test as 1 pending invitation and the newly created invitee row shows an incorrect/default role control, screenshot: docs/qa-browser-run-2026-05-13/screenshots/users-after-token-register.png. Expected: invite link loads a token-aware signup page, validates targeted email, creates the user, assigns invited role, grants appropriate app access, consumes the invitation, and does not allow unrelated public registration.
close_reason: Fixed invite URLs to use /register?token=..., added shared AllowThem invitation-token registration handling, verified invite acceptance assigns role/access and consumes invite.
